channel.title: IBD LitMonitor - Quality of Life & PROs
channel.description: IBD literature monitoring: Quality of Life & PROs

item.title: Specific Depressive Symptoms are Associated with Subtypes of Fatigue in Inflammatory Bowel Disease.
item.description: Journal of clinical psychology in medical settings - Fatigue is common in inflammatory bowel disease (IBD) yet firm understanding of its management remains unclear. While psychosocial factors influence fatigue, less is known about how these factors d...

item.title: Comment on "Understanding dietary beliefs, behaviors, and barriers in inflammatory bowel disease: a scoping review".
Turning off inflammation naturally via dual antioxidant and anti-inflammatory actions of chestnut wood extract through PPARγ and NF-κB pathways.
item.description: PloS one - Inflammatory bowel disease (IBD) arises from a persistent imbalance between oxidative stress and immune homeostasis, driving tissue injury and chronic intestinal inflammation. Natural polyphenols a...

item.title: Comment on 'Home-based transcutaneous electrical acupuncture-point stimulation for depressive symptoms in inflammatory bowel disease: a randomized feasibility study'.
Scutellaria baicalensis-Derived Extracellular Vesicles Alleviate Inflammatory Bowel Disease by Inhibiting the NF-κB/NLRP3 Pathway.
item.description: International journal of nanomedicine - Plant-derived extracellular vesicles (PDEV) are emerging as natural nanomedicines for various diseases. Scutellaria baicalensis (S. baicalensis) is a traditional Chinese herb long used to treat int...

item.title: The Role of Physical Activity and Physiotherapists in the Management of Inflammatory Bowel Disease: A Nationwide Cross-Sectional Survey.
item.description: Journal of clinical medicine - Background/Objectives: Inflammatory bowel diseases (IBDs) cause gastrointestinal symptoms that affect patients' quality of life. IBD improves with physical activity; however, fear of movement is a ...

item.title: Latent profiles of self-management behavior and associated factors among Chinese patients with ulcerative colitis.
item.description: Frontiers in public health - Ulcerative colitis (UC) poses a growing clinical and socioeconomic burden in China. Effective self-management is critical for long-term disease control, yet significant heterogeneity exists in self...

item.title: Preparation and Characterization of Rice Bran Protein Hydrolysates Enhanced via Alcalase and Lactiplantibacillus plantarum 13110 Co-Treatment: Antioxidant Properties and Ameliorative Effects on Ulcerative Colitis in Mice.
item.description: Nutrients - Ulcerative colitis, a chronic inflammatory disorder of the intestine, represents a major health concern worldwide. This study aimed to explore the in vivo efficacy of rice bran protein hydrolysates...

item.title: Physical, cognitive, and psychosocial fatigue are differently related to cortical complexity of superior temporal and frontal brain regions in Crohn's disease.
item.description: Frontiers in neuroimaging - Fatigue is common in persons with Crohn's disease, negatively impacting quality of life in both active and remitted disease state. Neural correlates of fatigue in Crohn's disease are understudied, ...

item.title: Development of Pseudoginsenoside RT2 as a Novel Gut-Selective Agent: Integrated Pharmacodynamic and Pharmacokinetic Evaluation of an Ocotillol Ginsenoside for Ulcerative Colitis.
item.description: Pharmaceuticals (Basel, Switzerland) - Background/Objectives: Ulcerative colitis is a chronic inflammatory bowel disease marked by a disrupted intestinal barrier and consequent aberrant immune responses. Pseudoginsenoside RT2, an ocotil...
